diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Cough is a common feature of an irritant-triggered airways exacerbation but is non-specific.

symptoms.symptom_name: Chest tightness with audible expiratory wheeze and breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Expiratory wheeze with chest tightness and breathlessness is the core of an asthma exacerbation.
An audible expiratory wheeze is a physical airflow-obstruction sign, not a feature of pure hyperventilation.

symptoms.symptom_name: Diffuse bilateral expiratory wheeze with prolonged expiratory phase on auscultation

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Auscultated diffuse expiratory wheeze confirms bronchospasm — a sealed clinician measurement the AI Doctor cannot obtain; it must infer airflow obstruction from the patient-reported whistle and work of breathing.

symptoms.symptom_name: Intercostal retractions / accessory-muscle use, RR 24, HR 110, SpO2 94%

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Objective tachypnoea, tachycardia, hypoxaemia and accessory-muscle use are severe-exacerbation criteria — sealed clinician measurements; the AI infers severity from reliever failure and visible work of breathing, not from a measured SpO2.

symptoms.symptom_name: Reliever (salbutamol) failure — heavy use with minimal relief

diagnostic_weight_edges.diagnosis: acute severe asthma / reactive-airways exacerbation, reliever-refractory
diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Reliever refractoriness is a marker of a severe exacerbation and impending deterioration.

symptoms.symptom_name: Widespread burning, excoriated erythematous rash on forearms, neck and face

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Burning, itchy, excoriated erythema on exposed skin after irritant contact is consistent with an acute irritant/contact dermatitis.
Skin involvement raises anaphylaxis but, without urticaria plus angioedema and circulatory/upper-airway involvement, does not support it.

symptoms.symptom_name: No tongue/lip/throat swelling, no stridor, no dysphagia; normotensive

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Absence of upper-airway angioedema, stridor and hypotension screens anaphylaxis negative as the driver.

symptoms.symptom_name: Acute onset following strong industrial chemical odour / increased local flaring

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A clear inhaled-irritant trigger with a temporal link supports an irritant-triggered airways exacerbation and dermatitis.

symptoms.symptom_name: Marked anxiety / distress about environmental (air and water) safety

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Genuine anxiety is present and comorbid, but it is a red herring for the acute cause — it must not down-rank the objective bronchospasm and hypoxia.

symptoms.symptom_name: Multi-year recurrent cough, chest tightness and skin irritation linked to residential environmental exposure

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: A chronic-exposure background with recurrent reactive-airway episodes supports an environmentally-driven asthma phenotype.

symptoms.symptom_name: Blood lead level, urinary VOC metabolites and systemic inflammatory markers

investigation_details.investigation_name: Environmental exposure panel (blood lead, urinary VOC metabolites, inflammatory markers)
investigation_details.expected_result: Pending — recommended by the source clinician for the usual GP to arrange
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: Non-urgent; relevant to the chronic exposure work-up, not the acute exacerbation. Not obtainable via telehealth in real time.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Supports the chronic-exposure context but does not change acute management or triage.

symptom_clusters.target_diagnosis: acute severe asthma / reactive-airways exacerbation, reliever-refractory
symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Wheeze/tight chest + objective severe-exacerbation signs + reliever failure together define a severe attack needing urgent escalation.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: SpO2 via a home pulse oximeter if the patient has one
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: If the patient can offer a home oximeter reading it helps gauge hypoxia, but its absence must NOT delay escalation — clinical severity (reliever failure, work of breathing) drives the decision.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: In-person severity assessment — auscultation, respiratory rate, SpO2, peak expiratory flow
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: The definitive severity assessment and treatment (nebulised bronchodilator, steroid, oxygen) require in-person/ED care that telehealth cannot deliver.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Environmental exposure panel (blood lead, urinary VOC metabolites, inflammatory markers)
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Appropriate for the chronic-exposure work-up via the usual GP; not part of the acute emergency response and must not distract from escalation.
